summary:
You are given an array of non-negative integers representing an elevation map, where each value is the height of a bar of width one. Imagine it rains and water collects in the dips between taller bars.

Compute how many units of water are trapped after the rain. Water sits above a position only up to the lower of the tallest bars to its left and to its right.

examples.explanation: The valleys formed by the taller bars on each side hold a combined total of 6 units of water.
Water pools in the gaps between the bars of heights 4 and 5, trapping 9 units in total.

constraints: An empty array traps 0 units of water
